Role in Inpatient Setting: Lead clinical team and oversee all medical treatment
Key Responsibilities: Diagnose mental illness, prescribe and manage medications, lead treatment team, perform psychiatric evaluations, authorize admissions and discharges

Role in Inpatient Setting: Conduct psychological testing and provide therapy
Key Responsibilities: Administer and interpret psychological assessments, provide individual and group therapy, consult on diagnosis, develop treatment plans

Role in Inpatient Setting: Prescribe medications and provide psychiatric care
Key Responsibilities: Evaluate patients, prescribe and adjust psychiatric medications, provide brief therapy, collaborate with treatment team

Role in Inpatient Setting: Provide 24/7 direct patient care, administer medications
Key Responsibilities: Monitor patient safety and vital signs, administer medications, document clinical observations, respond to psychiatric emergencies

Role in Inpatient Setting: Assist with direct patient care and medication administration
Key Responsibilities: Administer medications under supervision, monitor patient status, document care, support RNs in clinical tasks

Role in Inpatient Setting: Provide direct patient care and safety monitoring
Key Responsibilities: Supervise patients on milieu, de-escalate behavioral crises, document patient behavior, facilitate daily activities

Role in Inpatient Setting: Provide therapy and coordinate discharge planning
Key Responsibilities: Conduct psychosocial assessments, provide individual and family therapy, develop discharge plans, connect patients to community resources

Role in Inpatient Setting: Provide individual and family therapy
Key Responsibilities: Facilitate family therapy sessions, provide individual therapy, support patients in processing relationships and trauma, run group therapy

Role in Inpatient Setting: Provide individual and group therapy
Key Responsibilities: Conduct individual counseling sessions, lead psychoeducation groups, support treatment planning, provide crisis intervention

Role in Inpatient Setting: Restore daily functioning and life skills
Key Responsibilities: Assess functional abilities, teach coping and daily living skills, facilitate therapeutic activities, support transition to independence

Role in Inpatient Setting: Use recreation and leisure to support recovery
Key Responsibilities: Plan and lead therapeutic recreation programs, improve social skills through structured activities, assess leisure interests and abilities

Role in Inpatient Setting: Use visual art as a therapeutic medium
Key Responsibilities: Facilitate art therapy sessions, help patients express emotions non-verbally, process trauma through creative work, assess through art

Role in Inpatient Setting: Use music as a therapeutic intervention
Key Responsibilities: Design and lead music therapy sessions, use rhythm and sound to support emotional regulation, improve mood and engagement

Role in Inpatient Setting: Provide lived-experience support and mentorship
Key Responsibilities: Share personal recovery experience, provide hope and motivation, assist with daily coping strategies, support transition to community

Role in Inpatient Setting: Coordinate care across disciplines and plan discharge
Key Responsibilities: Coordinate services between providers, arrange post-discharge resources, verify insurance and benefits, connect patients to housing and support

Role in Inpatient Setting: Assess cognitive and brain-behavior functioning
Key Responsibilities: Administer neuropsychological tests, diagnose cognitive impairments, advise on treatment planning for patients with brain injuries or dementia

Role in Inpatient Setting: Address co-occurring substance use disorders
Key Responsibilities: Provide substance use counseling, facilitate 12-step and SMART Recovery groups, conduct substance use assessments, develop relapse prevention plans

Role in Inpatient Setting: Support spiritual and existential wellbeing
Key Responsibilities: Provide spiritual care and emotional support, facilitate religious observances, support patients and families through crisis and grief

Role in Inpatient Setting: Address nutritional needs linked to mental health
Key Responsibilities: Assess nutritional status, develop meal plans for patients with eating disorders or medication side effects, provide nutrition education

Role in Inpatient Setting: Use multi-modal arts for healing
Key Responsibilities: Integrate drama, dance, movement, and visual arts into therapy, support non-verbal expression of trauma and emotion
